reason: The text describes God offering David three catastrophic options through the prophet Gad: (1) three years of famine, (2) three months of military defeat, or (3) three days of plague with a destroying angel. David chooses the plague, and God sends a supernatural plague that kills 70,000 men from Israel ('seventy thousand men fell from Israel'). Additionally, a destroying angel is sent to devastate Jerusalem. These are clearly supernatural destructions ordained by Yahweh that affect the general population—not individual deaths but widespread public catastrophes affecting the nation of Israel and its capital.
